## Building a Modal Dialog in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A modal is a conditionally-rendered overlay plus three accessibility behaviours: close on Escape, close on backdrop click (but not content click), and lock background scroll while open. Keep it controlled with `v-model` so the parent owns the open state. ### Step 1 — Controlled with v-model Expose `modelValue` and emit `update:modelValue` to close. ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Structure with v-if and @click.self `@click.self` fires only when the backdrop itself is clicked — no manual `stopPropagation`. ```vue
...
``` ### Step 3 — Close on Escape Add the listener when opened, remove it when closed, using a `watch`. ```js watch(() => props.modelValue, (open) => { if (open) window.addEventListener('keydown', onKey); else window.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey); }); ``` ### Step 4 — Lock background scroll In the same `watch`, toggle `document.body.style.overflow`, and clean up on unmount. ```js document.body.style.overflow = open ? 'hidden' : ''; ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Content click closes | `@click.self` restricts to the backdrop. | | Listener leak | Remove on close and in `onUnmounted`. | | Focus | Production code traps focus and restores it on close. | **Interview tip:** `@click.self` is the idiomatic Vue answer to "backdrop closes, content doesn't" — reaching for it instead of manual `event.stopPropagation()` shows command of Vue's event modifiers.
